title: QUIC CRT dormant early-data attempt readiness architecture
purpose: Describe how the managed client/runtime path surfaces a narrow internal readiness observation from the dormant detached resumption carrier without opening early data or widening the public API.
risks: If the readiness observation omits either resumption credential material or the early-data prerequisite snapshot, the later 0-RTT family could overclaim readiness.
If the readiness observation is wired into public API, the slice widens the consumer promise instead of staying internal.
If the observation opens early data, the family stops being readiness-only and becomes a fake packet path.
If the dormant carrier is treated as equivalent to actual packet emission, the later emission family will inherit a false positive.
design_summary: The runtime now exposes one narrow internal readiness observation over the dormant detached carrier. The observation is true only when the carrier has both the resumption credential material already captured in the earlier slices and the early-data prerequisite material carried forward from REQ-QUIC-CRT-0139. The runtime still keeps `IsEarlyDataAdmissionOpen` explicitly false, so the readiness boundary is useful for the later packet-emission family without claiming that family yet.
alternatives_considered: Add actual 0-RTT packet emission in this family. Rejected because the send path and packet-format plumbing are not yet ready for a truthful emission claim.
Keep the carrier implicit and avoid a dedicated readiness observation. Rejected because the later family needs a traceable seam that is narrower than packet emission.
Promote the readiness observation into the public API. Rejected because the family must remain internal and managed-client only.
edge_cases_and_constraints: The slice stays client-role only and managed client/runtime only.
The readiness observation must remain false when the detached carrier lacks the `early_data` signal or the resumption credential material.
The accepted abbreviated resumption boundary from REQ-QUIC-CRT-0137 and the prerequisite capture from REQ-QUIC-CRT-0139 must remain unchanged.
No public API widening, `IsSupported` widening, key update claim, or anti-replay claim is allowed here.
data_and_state_considerations: The dormant detached carrier already stores the resumption ticket, nonce, lifetime, age add, capture timestamp, resumption master secret, ticket limit, and cloned peer transport parameters. The runtime only needs a narrow internal observation that sees those fields together and stays explicitly separate from actual 0-RTT packet construction or admission.

supplemental_sections.heading: Scope
supplemental_sections.content: This slice is limited to dormant early-data attempt readiness on the managed client/runtime path. It does not open early data, does not add 0-RTT packet handling, and does not widen any public resumption promise.
